titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas Chambers to the Under Secretary of Ireland, 22 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Young Chambers

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Thomas Young Chambers (1862-1940) to the under secretary of Ireland regarding the vacant position of clerk for the crown and peace of county Westmeath. Chambers states that he is a graduate of Trinity College and is a solicitor of thirty years standing.At the time this letter was written Sir Robert Chalmers (1858 - 1938) was serving as the under secretary of Ireland. Chalmers was sent to Ireland as a replacement for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO-RP-1916-5615-10595

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
UNIVERSITY CLUB
DUBLIN Bailieborough Co. Cavan 22nd June 1916 Sir As the post of Clerk of
the Crown & Peace for the County
of Westmeath is now vacant
I beg to apply for the appointment. I am a Solicitor of thirty
years standing and am a
graduate of Trinity College I shall be obliged if you
will place this application
before the proper authorities. Yours faithfully Thos.Y. Chambers The Under Secretary Dublin Castle
